2604.23546.title: COMO: Closed-Loop Optical Molecule Recognition with Minimum Risk Training
2604.23546.paper_summary: Optical chemical structure recognition (OCSR) translates molecular images into machine-readable representations like SMILES strings or molecular graphs, but remains challenging in real-world documents due to inexhaustible variations in chemical structures, shorthand conventions, and visual noise. Most existing deep-learning-based approaches rely on teacher forcing with token-level Maximum Likelihood Estimation (MLE). This training paradigm suffers from exposure bias, as models are trained under ground-truth prefixes but must condition on their own previous predictions during inference. Moreover, token-level MLE objectives hinder the optimization towards molecular-level evaluation criteria such as chemical validity and structural similarity. Here we introduce Minimum Risk Training (MRT) to OCSR and propose COMO (Closed-loop Optical Molecule recOgnition), a closed-loop framework that mitigates exposure bias by directly optimizing over molecule-level, non-differentiable objectives, by iteratively sampling and evaluating the model's own predictions. Experiments on ten benchmarks including synthetic and real-world chemical diagrams from patent and scientific literature demonstrate that COMO substantially outperforms existing rule-based and learning-based methods with less training data. Ablation studies further show that MRT is architecture-agnostic, demonstrating its potential for broad application to end-to-end OCSR systems.
2604.23546.paper_summary_zh: 光學化學結構識別 (OCSR) 將分子圖像轉換為機器可讀的表示形式，例如 SMILES 字串或分子圖，但在現實世界的文件中仍然面臨挑戰，因為化學結構、簡寫慣例和視覺噪聲的變化無窮無盡。大多數現有的基於深度學習的方法依賴於使用標記級最大似然估計 (MLE) 的教師強迫。這種訓練範式受到曝光偏見的困擾，因為模型是在真實前綴下進行訓練的，但在推斷過程中必須依賴自己之前的預測。此外，標記級 MLE 目標妨礙了對分子級評估標準（如化學有效性和結構相似性）的優化。在此，我們將最小風險訓練 (MRT) 引入 OCSR，並提出 COMO（閉環光學分子識別），這是一個閉環框架，通過直接優化分子級的非可微目標來減輕曝光偏見，並通過迭代抽樣和評估模型自己的預測。對十個基準的實驗，包括來自專利和科學文獻的合成和現實世界的化學圖表，證明 COMO 在訓練數據較少的情況下顯著優於現有的基於規則和基於學習的方法。消融研究進一步顯示 MRT 是架構無關的，顯示其在端到端 OCSR 系統中的廣泛應用潛力。
2604.23546.author: Zhuoqi Lyu et.al.
